blocks.body: A statement that two expressions are not equal — one is greater or less than the other. Symbols: > (greater than), < (less than), >= (greater than or equal to), <= (less than or equal to).
Solve inequalities like equations, with ONE key exception: when you multiply or divide both sides by a NEGATIVE number, FLIP the inequality sign. Example: -2x > 6. Divide by -2 and flip: x < -3.

blocks.label: Example 1
blocks.body: 3x + 5 > 14. Subtract 5: 3x > 9. Divide by 3: x > 3. Solution: all numbers greater than 3.

blocks.label: Example 2
blocks.body: -4x <= 20. Divide by -4 and FLIP sign: x >= -5.
Graphing on a number line: open circle (o) for > or < (does not include the endpoint). Closed circle (•) for >= or <= (includes the endpoint). Shade the direction of solutions.
The solution to an inequality is a range of values, not a single value. "x > 3" means infinitely many solutions: 4, 5, 3.1, 1000, etc.
